_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (retroactive rebuild from Codex Stage 3)
version_history: v3 (2026-05-17): retroactive editorial pass. Stage 3 (Codex) FAS=72 with 8 verified claims. SUBSTANTIAL HEADLINE CORRECTION: the RM20.7 billion figure is the financial-institution settlements subtotal, not the total recovery. MACC chief Azam Baki reported RM31.3 billion total recovery as of 2025, equal to 74.5% of the RM42 billion in 1MDB and SRC-linked losses. The issue is rebuilt around the correct total. Goldman Sachs settlement wording tightened to match Stage 3 correction (USD3.9B = USD2.5B cash + USD1.4B asset-recovery guarantee). 'Largest financial-crime recovery in Malaysian history' (UNVERIFIED) replaced with the verified MACC-anchored 74.5% ratio.

revision_log: CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Headline 'Reached Over RM20.7 Billion' → 'Reaches RM31.3 Billion, 74.5% of RM42B Losses'. The RM20.7B is the financial-institution settlements component; MACC's October 2025 total-recovery figure is RM31.3B per Azam Baki.
CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Hook 'surpassed RM20.7 billion' → 'reached RM31.3 billion, 74.5% of the RM42 billion MACC estimates was misappropriated' (proper total-vs-subtotal distinction).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Hook sub now clarifies that the RM20.73B financial-institution figure is a component of the total recovery, with Goldman / AmBank / JPMorgan named.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Fact 1 'Goldman Sachs paid USD3.9 billion in 2020' replaced with 'Goldman Sachs agreed to a USD3.9 billion settlement in 2020: USD2.5 billion cash plus a USD1.4 billion asset-recovery guarantee' per the correct settlement structure.

REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Fact 2 'and criminal prosecutions' dropped from the recovery-source list (Stage 3 said this should be tied to specific prosecutions with named amounts, which the cards do not have).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Fact 3 'USD4.5 billion (DOJ) up to RM30+ billion in broader Malaysian estimates' replaced with 'DOJ says more than USD4.5 billion was misappropriated. MACC's Malaysian estimate is RM42 billion in 1MDB and SRC-linked losses.'

REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Reframe 'whether the recovered funds reach public accounts' clarified to 'institutional reform follows' (Stage 3 noted funds were deposited into the Asset Recovery Trust Account; the live question is reform, not deposit).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — View 'RM20.7 billion is the largest financial-crime recovery in Malaysian history' replaced with verified 'RM31.3 billion recovered, 74.5% of estimated losses' per MACC chief Azam Baki 2025.

PRESERVED (Stage 3 VERIFIED): MOF October 2025 RM20.73B financial-institution settlement figure; USD2.5B + USD1.4B Goldman structure; DOJ USD1.4B return; multi-jurisdiction recoveries; ongoing litigation.
